purpose: Define the temporary-link scan used to identify unnecessary surfaces in an independent service and archive them without central runtime ownership.

scan_axes.question: Is this file, folder, or flow no longer used by the current runtime path?
Does this surface duplicate another active surface without providing a distinct operational role?
Does this surface still represent an old governance path that is no longer canonical?
Can this surface be archived without breaking the live runtime or the current governance kernel?

status_levels.meaning: No diet scan has been completed yet.
A first-pass classification scan is complete, but no archive-safe move is authorized yet.
A deeper scan has confirmed archive-safe candidates and a second pass may move local archive-safe surfaces.
A diet pass was attempted but required evidence or rollback notes are missing.

hard_rules: Service diet must reduce clutter without breaking the live runtime path.
A surface may be marked for archive only if the scan receipt and risk note both exist.
Central governance may direct the archive decision but must not take custody of the archived runtime itself.
The diet pass must never classify required DTP files or active temporary-link kit files as archive candidates.
Classification alone does not authorize deletion; archive movement remains service-local and receipt-gated.
